mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, leveraging advanced techniques for efficient memory management and request handling. Key trade-offs include the need for compatible hardware and potential complexity in setup for optimal performance.

steps.title: Add Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the vLLM Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.18.1

```

> This command adds the vLLM repository for version 0.18.1.

steps.title: Install Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
This step installs the necessary CRDs for vLLM to function correctly in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.18.1/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

```

> This command applies the CRDs required for vLLM.

steps.title: Install vLLM using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs vLLM in the specified namespace with the desired configuration.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.18.1

```

> This command installs vLLM in the 'vllm' namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource (CR)
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource to deploy a model using vLLM.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 1
EOF

```

> This command creates a sample vLLM resource for the 'gpt-3' model.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 1
EOF

steps.title: Verify the vLLM deployment
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the vLLM deployment to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> This command lists the vLLM resources in the 'vllm' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes vLLM from your cluster and warns about potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete crd vllms.vllm.io

```

> This command uninstalls vLLM but may leave behind orphaned resources.
> This command deletes the vLLM CRDs, which may result in data loss.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
This step includes backing up existing configurations, upgrading vLLM, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get vllm -n vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.18.1

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> Backup existing vLLM configurations.
> Upgrade vLLM to the specified version.
> Verify that the vLLM resources are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get vllm -n vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM resource may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Check the Helm installation logs for errors using 'helm status vllm --namespace vllm'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the model server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The model server may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Ensure the model is correctly specified in the Custom Resource and check the logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n vllm'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for vLLM.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits in the vLLM Custom Resource or scale your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CRD not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definitions may not have been installed.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.18.1/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new models and improved memory management features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the vLLM resource listed with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get vllm -n vllm'.
codeSnippets:

apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 1
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure your Kubernetes cluster meets the resource requirements and that you have the necessary tools installed.
